attacks.name: Collect Fire
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for 2 Fire Energy cards and attach them to Charizard (1 if there is only 1).

attacks.name: Flame Pillar
attacks.effect: You may discard a Fire Energy card attached to Charizard. If you do, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and do 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Charmeleon.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Charmander.

attacks.name: Spiral Growth
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to Rayquaza ex.

attacks.name: Dragon Burst
attacks.effect: Discard either all Fire Energy or all Lightning Energy attached to Rayquaza ex. This attack does 40 damage times the amount of Fire or Lightning Energy discarded.

rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Poison Breath
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Slimy Water
attacks.effect: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Colorless Energy in the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost (after applying effects to the Retreat Cost).

rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Melting Mountain
attacks.effect: Discard the top card from your deck. If that card is a basic Energy card, attach it to Magcargo ex.

attacks.name: Lava Flow
attacks.effect: You may discard any number of basic Energy cards attached to Magcargo ex when you use this attack. If you do, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage for each basic Energy card you discarded.

rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Stream
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to Latios ex.

attacks.name: Luster Purge

rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Mist Ball
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy and a Water Energy attached to Latias ex.

rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Genetic Memory
attacks.effect: Use any attack from Kingdra ex's Basic Pokémon card or Stage 1 Evolution card. (Kingdra ex doesn't have to pay for that attack's Energy cost.)

attacks.name: Hydrocannon
attacks.effect: Does 50 damage plus 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Kingdra ex but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 40 damage in this way.

rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Magnitude
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.effect: Golem ex does 50 damage to itself.

rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dragon Wave
attacks.effect: Discard a Water Energy card and a Lightning Energy card attached to Dragonite ex.

attacks.name: Giant Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Gigavolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Draw 3 cards. Then discard any 1 card from your hand.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play (excluding Pokémon-ex). Return that Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

Each Grass and Lightning Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) gets +10 HP.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

Each player pays Colorless less to retreat his or her Fire and Water Pokémon.

Search your discard pile for basic Energy cards. You may either show 1 basic Energy card to your opponent and put it into your hand, or show 3 basic Energy cards to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.

Attach Buffer Piece to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Damage done to the Pokémon Buffer Piece is attached to by an opponent's attack is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). At the end of your opponent's turn after you played Buffer Piece, discard Buffer Piece.

Attach Balloon Berry to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

When the Pokémon Balloon Berry is attached to retreats, discard Balloon Berry instead of discarding Energy cards.

attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 different types of basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Dragon Spark
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Pick On
attacks.effect: If the number of cards in your opponent's hand is at least 6, look at his or her hand. Choose a number of cards there until your opponent has 5 cards left in his or her hand and have your opponent shuffle the cards you chose into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Power Count
attacks.effect: Count the amount of Energy attached to all of your Pokémon and all of your opponent's Pokémon. If your Pokémon have less Energy than your opponent's, this attack does 20 damage plus 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Scrunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Shelgon during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Quick Touch

attacks.name: Magnetic Force
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Pokémon (including Magneton).

attacks.name: Synchronized Search
attacks.effect: If Girafarig and the Defending Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, pick any 1 card from your deck and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Breaking Impact
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage for each Colorless Energy in that Pokémon's Retreat Cost to that Pokémon (after applying effects to the Retreat Cost). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Special Circuit

attacks.name: Psychic Boom

attacks.name: Rainbow Burn

attacks.name: Dragon Dance

attacks.name: Dragon Song
attacks.effect: Each Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Bad News
attacks.effect: If the number of cards in your opponent's hand is at least 6, choose a number of cards there, without looking, until your opponent has 5 cards left. Have your opponent discard the cards you chose.

attacks.name: Prize Count
attacks.effect: If you have more Prize cards left than your opponent, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.
